div.div: Ariel generates step-by-step diagram walkthroughs to help humans understand complex systems. It is most useful in READMEs and pull request summaries
LLMs generate code much faster than engineers. Once upon a time, our bottleneck was how fast we could write code. Now, the bottleneck is reviewing AI-generated code
While the code AIs generate is usually valid on a low-level, is still useful (perhaps critical) for human engineers to comprehend and evaluate the architecture.
Automatically generating diagrams helps. Now, instead of building a mental model from scratch, the reviewer has a map to cross-reference against the code.
However, diagrams without narrative still leave reviewers context-switching — toggling between diagram and code, with no prescribed reading order.
Working memory has a hard ceiling. Overloaded reviewers rubber-stamp. Comprehending reviewers make better decisions.
ariel sequences the narrative — one idea per step, one visual change per idea. Working memory stays within its limits. Understanding builds incrementally.
